steps.title: Use the citation clue
steps.content: The sentence says that tracking how often other scholars refer to research is a way to judge its importance. The example of frequent citations shows that the blank needs a word meaning evaluate.

steps.title: Eliminate imprecise verbs
steps.content:
• Choice A means use, but citations do not use the importance of research.
  • Choice B means affect, but tracking citations reveals importance rather than changing it.
  • Choice C means weaken, which contradicts the claim that citations show significance.
The remaining choice means evaluate or judge. The answer is D.
